description: slack/publish_manifest MCP tool. 8KB publish cap. Replace semantics via pins.list/remove/add. 1/hour rate limit per channel. allowFrom publish gate. Optional agentCard shape matching A2A /.well-known/agent-card.json. Issue #31, ships v0.6.0 conditional. Blocked by 31-A.
title: Epic 31-B: Bot-manifest protocol — own-manifest publisher + A2A-compat (v0.6.0, conditional)

description: Tool accepts a manifest object, validates it via the same Zod schema as 31-A, and publishes it as a pinned message in the given channel. Only one manifest per bot per channel.
title: 31-B.1: slack/publish_manifest(channel, manifest) MCP tool

description: Publish manifest A. Publish manifest B. Assert: at no point are both A and B pinned simultaneously. Assert final state: only B pinned.
title: 31-B.10: Test — replace semantics: prior manifest unpinned before new one pinned

description: Document publish flow, 8KB cap, 1/h rate limit, allowFrom gate, replace semantics. Warn about publishing secrets (manifests are public to channel members).
title: 31-B.11: Docs — 000-docs/bot-manifest-protocol.md publisher-side section

description: Short doc block: why agentCard shape matches Google A2A. Migration path if A2A formalizes signed manifests (Slack pin becomes transport for a signed artifact). Defer actual signing to future epic.
title: 31-B.12: Docs — A2A alignment notes

description: Enforce 8KB on the serialized manifest at publish time. Stricter than 31-A's 40KB read cap (Postel's Law — strict on output, tolerant on input).
title: 31-B.2: Publish-size cap — 8KB (stricter than read)

description: Find any existing pinned manifest from this bot_id. pins.remove it. Post new manifest message. pins.add. Atomic-ish (no two manifests pinned at once, worst case transient gap).
title: 31-B.3: Replace semantics — pins.list → remove prior → post → pins.add

description: Map<channelId, lastPublishAt>. Reject second publish within 1h with clear error. Prevents pinning spam. Not persisted — restart resets.
title: 31-B.4: In-memory rate limiter — 1 publish per channel per hour

description: publish_manifest verifies the caller's user_id is in the top-level allowFrom set. Cross-references existing access control. Non-allowFrom caller rejected with clear error.
title: 31-B.5: Gate — only allowFrom callers may publish

description: Add optional agentCard:{schemas,endpoints,…} field to manifest schema. Shape follows Google A2A spec for future HTTP-level compat. Does not affect Slack pin flow.
title: 31-B.6: Optional agentCard field matching A2A /.well-known/agent-card.json

description: Full cycle. Publish via 31-B, read via 31-A, assert deep-equal (modulo publishedAt timestamp). Uses a real Slack mock / recorded response fixture.
title: 31-B.7: Test — round-trip publish → re-read returns equivalent manifest

description: Publish at t=0 succeeds. Publish at t=30min throws. Publish at t=61min succeeds. Advance fake clock to test.
title: 31-B.8: Test — rate limiter rejects second publish within 1h

description: Call publish_manifest as a user_id not in allowFrom. Assert rejection, assert no Slack API calls made, assert clear error message that does not leak allowFrom contents.
title: 31-B.9: Test — non-allowFrom caller rejected

description: Session lifecycle reference for #32 epics. Mermaid state diagram (activate → quiesce → deactivate) + sequence diagram for concurrent threads. Names Armstrong-style supervisor contract. Blocks Epic 32-A.
title: P0.2.3: 000-docs/session-state-machine.md (state + sequence diagrams)

description: JournalEvent type. SHA-256 hash chain (Schneier-Kelsey 1999). Redaction module for token patterns. Truncation. --audit-log-file CLI flag + SLACK_AUDIT_LOG env. Atomic append (O_APPEND). Zero Slack surface. Issue #30, ships v0.4.1 ahead of v0.5.0. Blocks Epic 30-B.
title: Epic 30-A: Local hash-chained audit log (v0.4.1 precursor)

description: TypeScript type + Zod schema. All audit events conform. kind is a tagged enum (tool_call_start, tool_call_end, policy_decision, session_activate, session_deactivate).
title: 30-A.1: JournalEvent type — {ts, kind, toolName, input, outcome, correlationId, prevHash, hash}

description: Write 100 events, flip 1 byte in entry 42's serialized payload, run verifier — must report chain break at 42. Also flip 1 byte in stored hash field — must report break.
title: 30-A.10: Test — tamper detection via chain verification

description: Explain Schneier-Kelsey chain. Show example 3-line log with prevHash chain. Include copy-paste verify one-liner (node -e '...' or a tiny verify script).
title: 30-A.11: Docs — 000-docs/audit-journal-architecture.md hash-chain section + verify command

description: Schneier-Kelsey 1999. Each line carries prevHash + its own hash. Boot: if log exists, read last line's hash; else prevHash = genesis constant. Single writer to prevent interleave.
title: 30-A.2: SHA-256 hash-chained writer — hash = sha256(prevHash || serialized(event))

description: ts = { wall: ISO-8601 UTC string, seq: monotonic uint64 }. Counter breaks ties inside same millisecond. Prevents adversarial re-ordering within a single clock tick.
title: 30-A.3: Trusted-anchor timestamp (ISO-8601 UTC + monotonic counter)

description: Pre-write transform. Replace matches with [REDACTED:type]. Types: anthropic, slack, github, aws. Pure function. Feeds 30-A writer AND 30-B full mode.
title: 30-A.4: Redaction module — regex matchers for sk-*, xoxb-*, ghp_*, AKIA*

description: Large inputs get collapsed. Pattern: first 200 chars + ' … (N chars omitted) … ' + last 200. Preserves correlation with tool error messages while bounding log size.
title: 30-A.5: Input truncation — first 200 + omitted + last 200 (>500 chars)

description: Opt-in. If unset, no journal writes. If set, validate path writable, open append-only handle at startup. Env var for deployment, flag for local runs.
title: 30-A.6: --audit-log-file CLI flag + SLACK_AUDIT_LOG env var

description: Use fs.createWriteStream with {flags:'a'}. fsync once per N events or on rotation. Rotation: size-based (e.g., 100MB → .log.1). Crash-safe.
title: 30-A.7: Atomic append (O_APPEND on POSIX) + fsync on rotation boundary

description: Generate 1000 events, write them, read back, recompute hash chain, assert identical to stored hashes. Proves writer's chain is correct.
title: 30-A.8: Test — hash chain verifies end-to-end over synthetic 1000-event log

description: Table test: each known token pattern goes in, [REDACTED:type] comes out. Negative cases: random hex does not get redacted. Include edge cases (tokens inside JSON strings).
title: 30-A.9: Test — redaction catches all documented token patterns

description: Block Kit threaded replies with correlation IDs. Per-channel audit: off|compact|full. Self-echo regression lock-in for allowBotIds. Rate-limit batching. Graceful failure — Slack API errors never block tool exec. Issue #30, ships v0.5.1 (not SOC2 audit — reframed as operator visibility). Blocked by 30-A, 32-B, 29-B.
title: Epic 30-B: Action journal — Slack-thread projection (operator visibility)

description: New optional field on per-channel policy. Default 'off' (no projection). 'compact' = tool name + outcome. 'full' = redacted inputs + outcome.
title: 30-B.1: Add audit:'off'|'compact'|'full' field to ChannelPolicy in lib.ts

description: Full flow: policy require:1 → Block Kit posts → approve → tool executes → receipt edited. Correlation ID in both Block Kit context and receipt context. Assert match.
title: 30-B.10: Test — correlation ID links receipt to permission-relay prompt (requires 29-B)

description: Formal reference. Enumerate off/compact/full semantics. Warn about PII in full mode. Link 000-docs/audit-journal-architecture.md for the local-log counterpart.
title: 30-B.11: Docs — ACCESS.md audit field documentation

description: Explain: 30-B Slack projection is operator visibility, 30-A local log is authoritative. Slack messages may be rate-limited or lost; local log is hash-chained and tamper-evident.
title: 30-B.12: Docs — CLAUDE.md projection vs authoritative log section

description: Before tool execution, if audit!=off, post a stub threaded reply with :receipt: emoji + correlation ID in Block Kit context block. Store its ts for edit.
title: 30-B.2: Pre-execution hook — threaded :receipt: post with correlation ID

description: After tool execution, chat.update the receipt with exit code, path, summary. Edit (not new message) to keep thread clean. Handle rate-limit on update path.
title: 30-B.3: Post-execution hook — edit receipt with outcome

description: Renders: 'tool: <name>  →  <outcome>'. Used when inputs could contain user context that shouldn't be echoed back into the channel.
title: 30-B.4: compact mode — tool name + outcome only (no inputs)

description: Pulls redaction module from 30-A. Renders: 'tool: <name>\ninputs: <redacted>\noutcome: <x>'. Full mode requires opt-in because of the higher info density.
title: 30-B.5: full mode — redacted inputs + outcome (uses 30-A redaction)

description: If chat.postMessage / chat.update fails during projection, log and continue. NEVER block tool execution. This is projection — the authoritative log is 30-A's local file.
title: 30-B.6: Graceful failure — Slack API error on journal post is log-only

description: Token bucket per channel. If burst exceeds 5/s, collapse to a single summary message linking to the local log path. Prevents Slack rate-limit outages from audit traffic.
title: 30-B.7: Rate-limit batching — >5 events/sec collapses to summary

description: Two-bot channel with allowBotIds. Bot A's projection messages must not trigger Bot A's gate() inbound. Locks in the fix from PR #33.
title: 30-B.8: Regression test — self-echo on allowBotIds channels stays filtered (locks in #33)

description: Default state. Submit tool call. Assert chat.postMessage is never called for a receipt. Local log (30-A) should still be written if --audit-log-file is set.
title: 30-B.9: Test — audit:off produces zero Slack messages

description: Audit journal reference for #30 epics. Mermaid sequence diagram (tool call → hash chain → projection). Separates local hash-chained log from Slack projection. Notes Schneier-Kelsey hash chain (1999), Dapper sampling. Blocks Epic 30-A.
title: P0.2.5: 000-docs/audit-journal-architecture.md (sequence diagram)

description: Create 000-docs/THREAT-MODEL.md. Four principals: session owner, Claude process, human approver, peer agent. Attack surface per primitive. Mermaid trust-boundary diagram. Elevates private gist content into repo. Blocks P0.3.
title: P0.2.2: 000-docs/THREAT-MODEL.md with trust boundary diagram

description: Create ARCHITECTURE.md at repo root. Names four-principal model, session boundary, policy evaluator, journal sink, manifest consumer. Mermaid top-level component diagram. Blog-post commitment. Blocks Epic 32-A.
title: P0.2.1: ARCHITECTURE.md with top-level component diagram

description: Wire evaluate() into server permission-relay. Multi-approver state machine (NIST two-person integrity, user_id dedup). UTC clock injection. Startup warnings for unsafe rules. Emit policy.decision trace events for Epic 30-A consumption. Issue #29, ships v0.5.0. Blocked by 29-A, 32-B. Blocks 30-B.
title: Epic 29-B: Declarative policy engine — relay integration + multi-approver

description: Wire evaluate() call at the top of the permission-relay handler (~L876 — verify at implementation time). All four decision tags must be handled downstream.
title: 29-B.1: Consult evaluate() at permission-relay entry in server.ts

description: Policy: {decision:'deny',reason:'write_file blocked by policy'}. Assert thread receives reason, tool is NOT executed, no Block Kit.
title: 29-B.10: Integration test — deny surfaces reason in thread, no exec

description: Every evaluate() call emits one structured log line with decision tag, match info, correlationId. Epic 30-A journal consumes these.
title: 29-B.11: Emit policy.decision trace events (stdout structured log)

description: Document auto_approve / deny / require decisions, policy schema, monotonicity check, shadow linter. Link issue #29.
title: 29-B.12: CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] — policy engine entries

description: If decision.tag==='auto_approve', skip permission prompt and execute the tool. Emit policy.decision trace event (feeds 30-A journal). Log structured line.
title: 29-B.2: auto_approve branch — bypass Block Kit, emit decision trace

description: If decision.tag==='deny', post decision.reason as a threaded reply and refuse execution. Emit policy.decision trace. No Block Kit.
title: 29-B.3: deny branch — post reason to thread, no prompt, no exec

description: Allocate pending approval state. Requires Epic 32-B widened pairing key. Multi-approver count tracked until quorum or timeout.
title: 29-B.4: require branch — pending-approvers map keyed (thread_ts, requestId)

description: NIST two-person integrity — same human can't double-satisfy quorum. Key on Slack user_id from the approval event, never on display_name (spoofable).
title: 29-B.5: Approver dedup on verified user_id (NOT display name)

description: Pass a () => Date clock into evaluate(). Prod uses () => new Date(). Tests inject fixed time. No calls to new Date() inside evaluate().
title: 29-B.6: UTC clock injection for auto_approve.window

description: On load, scan policies: any rule with no match predicates AND auto_approve decision → loud warning. Likely misconfig (matches everything).
title: 29-B.7: Startup warning — log rules with empty match + auto_approve

description: Full server test. Policy: {match:{tool:'read_file',pathPrefix:'/safe'},decision:'auto_approve'}. Fire tool call; assert NO Block Kit Slack message, tool result returned.
title: 29-B.8: Integration test — auto_approve for read_file /safe does NOT prompt

description: Policy: {decision:'require',approvers:2}. Approver A clicks twice — tool does NOT fire. Approver A + approver B click once each — tool fires.
title: 29-B.9: Integration test — require:2 needs two distinct user_ids

description: Policy evaluation reference for #29 epics. Mermaid flowchart (gate → match → combine → decision). Names first-applicable combining algorithm (XACML) and rule-shadowing detection. Blocks Epic 29-A.
title: P0.2.4: 000-docs/policy-evaluation-flow.md (flowchart)

description: Bot manifest reference for #31 epics. Mermaid sequence diagram (publish → pin → read) + 'advertisements are not grants' invariant box. Names Miller 2006 / Google A2A alignment. Blocks Epic 31-A.
title: P0.2.6: 000-docs/bot-manifest-protocol.md (sequence + invariant box)

description: Zod schema with magic header __claude_bot_manifest_v1__. slack/read_peer_manifests MCP tool. Size caps (40KB). Silent-drop on malformed. Invariant: manifest data NEVER passed to policy evaluate (Miller 2006: advertisements are not grants). Cached 5-min. Issue #31, ships v0.6.0 conditional on A2A adoption or Slack signed-message primitive. Blocks Epic 31-B.
title: Epic 31-A: Bot-manifest protocol — read-only peer consumer (v0.6.0, conditional)

description: Fields: __claude_bot_manifest_v1__:true, agent:{id,name,version}, capabilities:string[], contact:string, publishedAt:ISO-8601. Header acts as discriminator for pins.list filter.
title: 31-A.1: Zod schema with magic header __claude_bot_manifest_v1__

description: Explain the pin-as-manifest convention, header magic, size caps, the Miller invariant. Reference Google A2A /.well-known/agent-card.json for alignment.
title: 31-A.10: Docs — 000-docs/bot-manifest-protocol.md consumer-side section

description: Flow: pins.list(channel) → filter to messages containing the magic header → parse JSON → validate via Zod → collect. Returns [] if no pins, partial on some-valid/some-invalid.
title: 31-A.2: slack/read_peer_manifests(channel) MCP tool

description: If a pinned message body exceeds 40KB at the JSON parse step, drop it without error propagation. Log a debug line. Prevents memory-bomb DoS via oversized pin.
title: 31-A.3: Size cap — 40KB per manifest on read (silent drop)

description: Miller 2006: 'advertisements are not grants.' The peer's manifest is advertising — it can claim anything. Policy decisions MUST use only verified signals (user_id, channel, tool). Add a type-level guard if possible.
title: 31-A.4: Invariant — manifest data NEVER passed to evaluate() policy

description: In-memory LRU keyed on channelId → (cachedAt, manifests). On read, return cache if <5min, else refetch. Prevents pins.list spam.
title: 31-A.5: Rate limit on read — 1 fetch per channel per 5 min (cached)

description: Pin a message containing the magic header but broken JSON. read_peer_manifests must return [] (or skip that entry), not throw. Verify via error capture.
title: 31-A.6: Test — malformed JSON dropped silently, does not crash tool

description: Pin 3 messages: valid A, schema-invalid B (wrong type in capabilities[]), valid C. Tool returns [A, C], drops B silently, logs debug.
title: 31-A.7: Test — schema-invalid entry dropped, other valid entries returned

description: Pin a message that passes schema but body is 50KB. Tool must drop it at the 40KB size cap, before schema validation. Assert [].
title: 31-A.8: Test — oversized manifest dropped

description: Negative test. Attempt to call evaluate(call, policies, now) where call.manifestClaims is populated. Compile-time error if possible via type. Runtime assert if not.
title: 31-A.9: Test — policy engine (29-A) refuses manifest data as input

description: Pure policy.ts module. Zod schema for PolicyRule. PolicyDecision tagged union. evaluate() with first-applicable combining (XACML). Path canonicalization via realpath. Load-time shadow-detection linter. Monotonicity invariant check. Zero server wiring. Issue #29, ships v0.5.0. Blocks Epic 29-B.
title: Epic 29-A: Declarative policy engine — evaluation core

description: Schema: { match: { tool?, pathPrefix?, from? }, one of decision:'auto_approve'+window?:UTCHours, decision:'deny'+reason:string, decision:'require'+approvers:number+from:string[] }. Lives in new policy.ts.
title: 29-A.1: Zod schema for PolicyRule (auto_approve/deny/require variants)

description: type PolicyDecision = {tag:'auto_approve',trace}|{tag:'deny',reason,trace}|{tag:'require',approvers,from,trace}|{tag:'prompt',trace}. Returned by evaluate().
title: 29-A.2: PolicyDecision tagged union

description: Iterate rules in order, return first match's decision. Default to 'prompt' if no match. Pure function — no side effects. Takes clock parameter for testability.
title: 29-A.3: evaluate(call, policies, now) — first-applicable combining (XACML)

description: Reuse lib.ts assertSendable realpath pattern. Canonicalize both rule pathPrefix and request path before compare. Traversal attempts rejected before match.
title: 29-A.4: Path canonicalization for match.pathPrefix via realpath (CWE-22)

description: Flag narrow deny rules placed after broad auto_approve rules (unreachable). Emit structured warning list at load. Fail-warn, not fail-closed.
title: 29-A.5: Load-time linter detectShadowing(policies) — warnings

description: Assert no rule widens trust beyond the top-level allowFrom set. Reject config at load if any 'from' in auto_approve or require references user_id outside allowFrom. Fail-closed.
title: 29-A.6: Monotonicity invariant check at load — fail-closed

description: Cover: match precedence, UTC window gating, deny message propagation, multi-approver count, dedup-by-user_id (verified id not display name), traversal rejection, malformed entry skip, shadow detection, monotonicity violation rejection. Derived from issue #29 body.
title: 29-A.7: 15+ test cases for evaluate() — full matrix

description: 3-4 concrete policy configs with expected decisions. Shows the first-applicable walk. Cross-link from ACCESS.md.
title: 29-A.8: Docs — worked examples in 000-docs/policy-evaluation-flow.md

description: Formal schema reference (Zod → doc). Field-by-field. Note monotonicity constraint. Placeholder until 29-B lands runtime wiring.
title: 29-A.9: Docs — ACCESS.md policy-schema section

description: SessionSupervisor actor (Armstrong-style): activate, quiesce, deactivate. Widen outbound gate from channel to (channel, thread_ts). Widen permission-pairing key. Idle reaper (default 4h). slack/list_sessions MCP tool. Route peer-bot messages via supervisor. Issue #32, ships v0.5.0. Blocked by 32-A. Blocks 29-B, 30-B.
title: Epic 32-B: Thread-scoped sessions — supervisor + lifecycle

description: Write interface comment block naming activate/quiesce/deactivate contract. Reference Joe Armstrong's supervision tree. Lives in lib.ts or a new supervisor.ts if the module grows large.
title: 32-B.1: Design doc — SessionSupervisor interface (Armstrong 2003)

description: Post-#33 peer-bot messages must land in the correct session. Update gate() delivery path to dispatch via supervisor.activate(key) + submit. Respects allowBotIds per-channel opt-in.
title: 32-B.10: Route peer-bot messages into thread's session via supervisor

description: Start session, begin tool call, advance clock past idle threshold. Reaper tick must NOT deactivate while in-flight map has entries. Deactivation happens only after tool call completes.
title: 32-B.11: Test — reaper respects in-flight tool calls

description: Describe the supervisor lifecycle in ARCHITECTURE.md. Reference session-state-machine.md for the diagrams. Note the Armstrong-style discipline.
title: 32-B.12: Docs — reaper + supervisor section in ARCHITECTURE.md

description: On activate: load session from disk via loader, allocate in-flight Map<requestId, AbortController>. Emit structured 'session.activate' log line with channel, thread, ownerId.
title: 32-B.2: Impl activate(key) — read state, register in-flight tool calls map

description: quiesce: stop accepting new tool calls for this key, wait for in-flight map to drain, emit 'session.quiesce' log. Idempotent.
title: 32-B.3: Impl quiesce(key) — refuse new work, await in-flight

description: deactivate: call persist() via atomic writer, release handle, remove from active map. Emit 'session.deactivate' log. Guaranteed to run after quiesce.
title: 32-B.4: Impl deactivate(key) — persist, release, emit close

description: Start two sessions (same channel, different threads). Submit tool call in thread A. Assert that completion post lands ONLY in thread A. Current code would let it leak to thread B.
title: 32-B.5: Failing test — tool call from thread A cannot post to thread B

description: Update lib.ts:assertOutboundAllowed to match on tuple not just channel. Update callers in server.ts. Security-critical (outbound gate layer).
title: 32-B.6: Widen assertOutboundAllowed to (channel, thread_ts) tuple

description: Update permission-relay handler in server.ts (~L876 — verify current line). Key prevents a Block Kit approval from one thread satisfying a permission ask from another thread.
title: 32-B.7: Widen permission-pairing key from requestId to (thread_ts, requestId)

description: setInterval tick that finds sessions whose lastActiveAt exceeds threshold. Calls quiesce() then deactivate(). Configurable via env var. Skips sessions with in-flight work.
title: 32-B.8: Idle reaper tick — SLACK_SESSION_IDLE_MS, default 4h

description: Register new MCP tool returning list of active (channel, thread_ts, lastActiveAt). Bounded output. DOES NOT return session state bodies (security — state may carry secrets).
title: 32-B.9: MCP tool slack/list_sessions — introspection

description: Add four-principal threat-model section to SECURITY.md (session owner, Claude process, human approver, peer agent). Names invariants every later bead must respect. Cross-links 000-docs/THREAT-MODEL.md. Blocks all eight feature epics.
title: P0.3: Promote four-principal model into SECURITY.md, cross-link THREAT-MODEL.md

description: Define what a session IS and where it lives on disk. Pure data-model: SessionKey, Session type, sessionPath with realpath guard, atomic writer, loader, migrator for flat→nested layout. No runtime behavior yet. Issue #32, ships v0.5.0. Blocks Epic 32-B.
title: Epic 32-A: Thread-scoped sessions — identity + storage

description: Add SessionKey = { channelId: string; threadTs: string | null } to lib.ts. Pure type — no implementation. Used by sessionPath() and supervisor.
title: 32-A.1: Define SessionKey type in lib.ts

description: Add link from CLAUDE.md Security Architecture block (the section listing five defense layers) to the session-state-machine reference doc.
title: 32-A.10: Docs — cross-link 000-docs/session-state-machine.md from CLAUDE.md

description: Session = state blob + metadata (createdAt, lastActiveAt, ownerId). Placed in lib.ts alongside SessionKey. Pure type.
title: 32-A.2: Define Session type

description: Write failing test first (TDD). Proves current flat layout cannot distinguish sibling threads. Covers the bug the epic fixes.
title: 32-A.3: Failing test — two threads in one channel produce two distinct session files

description: Reject '..' in thread_ts. Normalize channelId. Guard against path traversal (CWE-22). Reuse the realpath pattern from lib.ts assertSendable().
title: 32-A.4: Impl sessionPath(root, key) with realpath-safe joining

description: Write to tmp sibling file, chmod 0o600, then rename to final. Matches .env token-security pattern. No partial writes visible.
title: 32-A.5: Atomic session writer: tmp + chmod 0o600 + rename

description: Reuse assertSendable realpath pattern (lib.ts ~L189). Refuse to read through symlinks out of state root. Fail-closed on resolution error.
title: 32-A.6: Session loader with symlink-resolution guard

description: Detect legacy layout, move file into new directory, preserve mtime. Idempotent. Run at startup if legacy files detected.
title: 32-A.7: One-shot migrator — flat <channel>.json → <channel>/default.json

description: Integration test: write under legacy layout, restart, read under new layout. Write under new layout, restart, read back. Both must round-trip.
title: 32-A.8: Test — state survives process restart under both layouts

description: Show ~/.claude/channels/slack/<channel>/<thread_or_default>.json layout. Explain migrator. Note 0o600 permission.
title: 32-A.9: Docs — update ACCESS.md state-layout section with new directory tree
